status=open
reason=Bash tool is unusable in this execution environment — every invocation (including trivial commands like `pwd` and `echo test`) fails with `ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/...'` while creating its own sandbox working directory. This is a host/container-level disk exhaustion issue, not a repository or code issue. Read/Edit/Write tools function normally (confirmed against cli/internal/server/server.go), but there is no way to run `go build`, `go test ./...`, `git add`/`git commit`, or `lefthook run pre-commit` without Bash. The bead contract requires all of these before a commit, and forbids committing red/unverified code, so no code changes were made or committed this run. This is retryable once the execution host has free disk space (or the Bash sandbox tmp path is cleared) — the actual work (replacing `*bead.Store` in cli/internal/server/server.go:929 and cli/internal/server/server.go:5269 with `bead.Backend` or the narrowest fitting backend sub-interface) was not started for any reason other than this environment blocker.
suggested_action=retry with a smart agent
